question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted environment variable `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_BUCKETNAME` pointing to an attacker-controlled cloud bucket containing blocks with tampered custom fee schedules, causing the importer to ingest fraudulent fee data as if it were canonical?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fallback logic in getBucketName() — where a blank bucketName causes the network-derived bucket name to be used — by poisoning the network property via environment variable `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_NETWORK`, redirecting block downloads to a bucket containing blocks with modified transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the Spring Boot external configuration (e.g., via a misconfigured config server or mounted ConfigMap) set bucketName to an attacker-controlled GCS/S3 bucket, causing the importer to process blocks with artificially zeroed transaction fees, leading to incorrect fee accounting in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: sourceType] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `hiero.mirror.importer.block.sourceType` property (e.g., via environment variable `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_SOURCETYPE=BLOCK_NODE`) to force the importer to consume blocks exclusively from attacker-controlled block nodes that serve blocks with inflated or zeroed transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: sourceType] Can an unprivileged external user set sourceType to AUTO via external configuration, then inject a malicious block node into the nodes list, causing the CompositeBlockSource to prefer the attacker's node and ingest blocks with modified fee schedules outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malicious entry into the `hiero.mirror.importer.block.nodes` collection via environment variable (e.g., `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_NODES_0_HOST=attacker.host`) with priority=0 (highest), causing the importer to stream blocks from the attacker's node containing transactions with fees modified outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user set a block node entry with `requiresTls=false` and a malicious host, bypassing TLS verification, and serve blocks with tampered fractional fee numerators/denominators that the importer ingests without detecting the modification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: autoDiscoveryEnabled] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the autoDiscoveryEnabled=true default by poisoning the block node discovery cache or database with attacker-controlled node entries, causing the importer to auto-discover and stream from a malicious node that serves blocks with modified transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: autoDiscoveryEnabled] Can an unprivileged external user disable autoDiscoveryEnabled via external config to prevent legitimate block nodes from being discovered, then inject a static malicious node via the nodes list, causing all fee-bearing transactions to be ingested from the attacker's source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user replace the `hiero.mirror.importer.block.ledger.historyProofVerificationKey` with an attacker-controlled key via environment variable, causing TSS block verification to accept attacker-signed blocks containing transactions with fees modified outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.block.ledger.ledgerId` to an attacker-controlled value, causing the importer to accept blocks from a different ledger that contains fee schedules with values outside the design parameters of the legitimate network?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: ledger] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `nodeContributions` list in LedgerProperties to include attacker-controlled nodes with inflated weights, causing the TSS threshold to be met by attacker nodes alone, allowing acceptance of blocks with tampered fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: frequency] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.block.frequency` to an extremely low value (e.g., 1ms) via external config, causing a polling storm that creates race conditions in fee transaction processing, leading to duplicate or out-of-order fee records in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: frequency] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to an extremely large value, causing the importer to stop polling for new blocks, effectively freezing the fee schedule state at an outdated value and causing the mirror node to serve stale fee data to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.block.stream.maxBlockItems` to the minimum allowed value (1000) via external config, causing blocks containing fee schedule update transactions to be rejected as oversized, preventing legitimate fee updates from being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.block.stream.maxStreamResponseSize` to a very small value, causing the importer to truncate block stream responses that contain fee-bearing transactions, resulting in incomplete fee data being persisted to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.block.stream.maxSubscribeAttempts` to 1 via external config, causing the importer to permanently mark legitimate block nodes as inactive after a single transient error, forcing fallback to a less-verified file source where fee data may be tampered?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.block.stream.readmitDelay` to a very large value (e.g., days), causing a temporarily-failed legitimate block node to never be readmitted, permanently routing block ingestion through a less-secure path where fee modifications go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: stream] Can an unprivileged external user set `hiero.mirror.importer.block.stream.responseTimeout` to an extremely low value (100ms minimum), causing legitimate block nodes to time out and be marked unhealthy, forcing the importer to fall back to a file source that an attacker has pre-populated with blocks containing modified fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: enabled] Can an unprivileged external user toggle `hiero.mirror.importer.block.enabled` from false to true via external config, activating the block stream source prematurely before proper ledger verification keys are configured, causing the importer to accept unverified blocks with arbitrary fee values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: persistBytes] Can an unprivileged external user enable `hiero.mirror.importer.block.persistBytes` via external config, causing raw block bytes (including fee transaction data) to be stored in the database, and then exploit a separate read path to extract and analyze fee structures to craft targeted fee manipulation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: writeFiles] Can an unprivileged external user enable `hiero.mirror.importer.block.writeFiles` via external config, causing verified block stream files (including fee transaction records) to be written to the filesystem, and then exploit a path traversal or symlink attack to redirect writes to a location that overwrites fee-related configuration files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StringUtils.isNotBlank(bucketName) check by setting bucketName to a whitespace-only string, causing the condition to evaluate to false and falling back to the network-derived bucket name, which the attacker has already redirected via network property manipulation to a bucket with tampered fee data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getBucketName() has no validation on the returned bucket name string, allowing injection of a bucket name containing path traversal sequences or special characters that redirect the cloud storage client to an attacker-controlled endpoint serving blocks with modified fees?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Field: nodes] Can an unprivileged external user set a block node with priority=0 and requiresTls=false pointing to a man-in-the-middle proxy, intercepting the block stream and modifying fee-related block items (FixedFee, FractionalFee, RoyaltyFee) in transit before they reach the importer?
